notes: $38B revenue in 1997. Acquired Compaq 2002 for $25B. Split into HP Inc. and HPE in 2015.
$20B revenue in 1997. Acquired Tandem Computers 1997. Acquired DEC 1998. Acquired by HP 2002 for ~$25B.
HP co-announced Simplified Enterprise Computing with Bill Gates in March 1997. HP endorsed Exchange Server, IIS, Wolfpack clustering, ZAW initiative.
Non-Stop fault-tolerant systems. Acquired by Compaq 1997. Tandem handled 70% of world monetary transactions, 66% ATM/credit card, 90% stock transactions. NonStop eventually became HPE NonStop.
$76B revenue in 1997. Cited as competing with HP for enterprise NT; also offered Lotus Notes/Domino as Exchange alternative.
Cited as competitive pressure on Compaq via direct-sales no-middleman model. Direct model had "leap-frogged" Compaq.
$15B revenue in 1997. Acquired by Compaq for $9.6B in June 1998.
HP co-developing IA-64 (Merced) with Intel; HP expected early chip access advantage.
